meaning: adj.(土地)未加改良的,未耕种的
examples: But the main building remained unimproved.
They are but improved means to an unimproved end, an end which it was already but too easy to arrive at;
For example, don't forget to order self-leveling outriggers if you're using your RT scissor lifts on unimproved surfaces.
Hominy Ridge was nothing but an out-of-date, unimproved, one-room country schoolhouse in the backwoodsiest corner of Indiana.
George wanted to tax the rental value of unimproved land, as that was the least distortionary tax possible.
